feature: LLM query router with keyword-fallback contract
story: As an analyst, I want free text routed to the right graph walks so that I don't have to know capability names.
expected: POST /api/query/route (text 1-500 chars) LLM-classifies to ≤3 registry capability ids, best first, each with a short reason; ids validated against the live registry — hallucinated or duplicate ids filtered, never surfaced; disabled capabilities may be recommended (UI explains why unavailable). Contract: on ANY failure (no provider key, timeout, unparseable reply) the endpoint returns {"candidates": [], "source": "keyword"} and the client falls back to its local keyword router (routing.ts rankQueryCapabilities) — routing never gets worse than the deterministic path, and a lane fault never 5xxs. LIGHT preset; 20/min NL rate limit; auth-gated.
trigger: analyst submits free text in the query bar
test_result: Pass — test_query_overlay.py + test_query_accepted_links.py 7/7 + routing.test.ts green, run 2026-07-02 offline (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY cleared)
notes: Fault-isolated LLM lane (degrade-to-keyword pattern): keyless deploys stay deterministic; /capabilities exposes availability.model_lane so the frontend hides the affordance entirely.
edge_cases: keyless → keyword contract without an LLM call; model returns unknown/duplicate ids → filtered; >3 candidates → capped at 3; LLM exception → warn log + keyword contract (never 5xx); rate limit → 429; invalid: empty or >500-char text → 422 (RouteRequest min 1 / max 500)
test_cases: test_query_overlay.py::test_route_filters_to_registry (hallucinated id dropped, capped at 3, source=llm), ::test_available_false_when_keyless; routing.test.ts: notes/memo language ranks analyst-memos first

feature: Citation-gated model overlay on the deterministic graph
story: As an analyst, I want model-proposed links and commentary over a graph without the model ever touching the deterministic payload so that the overlay stays defensible and reproducible.
expected: POST /api/query/overlay {capability_id (1-64), issuer_id? (≤36), force?} builds the deterministic graph, retrieves ≤12 vault chunks, and asks the HEAVY-preset model for ≤6 links between EXISTING node ids — an edge with a hallucinated endpoint, self-loop, already-drawn pair, duplicate, or no valid citation is dropped, not drawn; confidence coerced into High/Medium/Low; ≤3 suggested walks filtered to enabled registry ids. The validated artifact is persisted to query_overlays (migration 0019: model id + payload + analyst_id + graph_hash) and graph_hash is the cache key — an unchanged graph returns the frozen artifact (cached: true), force=true bypasses. Empty graph → no LLM spend, nothing persisted. Keyless → 503; LLM/parse failure → 502 with detail, /graph unaffected. Overlay edges live outside the deterministic graph payload, so table/CSV/print never include them. 10/min rate limit; auth-gated.
trigger: analyst toggles the model overlay on a rendered graph
test_result: Pass — test_query_overlay.py + test_query_accepted_links.py 7/7 green, run 2026-07-02 offline
notes: max_tokens 2000: a truncated reply fails validation and is discarded fail-closed (seen live on DeepSeek at 900); overlay is never on the /graph path, so the fault-isolation property holds.
edge_cases: unknown capability → 404; keyless → 503; unparseable/truncated JSON reply → 502, deterministic graph unaffected; uncited edge → dropped, not drawn; identical graph rerun → cached artifact, no second LLM call; empty graph → short-circuit (no spend, no row); invalid: capability_id empty/>64 or issuer_id >36 → 422 (OverlayRequest)
test_cases: test_query_overlay.py::test_overlay_grounding_gate_and_cache (hallucinated endpoint + uncited edge dropped; rerun serves cached artifact), ::test_overlay_unparseable_reply_raises (502 path), ::test_overlay_empty_graph_skips_llm (no spend, no persist)
